codes.rationale: Presenile dementia with depression is a comorbidity pattern where depression is secondary to dementia, not primary depression.
Senile dementia with depression is a comorbidity pattern where depression is secondary to dementia, not primary depression.

codes.term: Single episode of major depression in full remission (disorder)
codes.rationale: Single episode of major depression in full remission describes a resolved state; codes for resolved conditions should typically be excluded.
Major depression in partial remission describes a resolved or resolving state; codes for remission should typically be excluded.
Major depression in remission describes a resolved or resolving state; codes for remission should typically be excluded.
Major depression in full remission describes a resolved state; codes for resolved conditions should typically be excluded.
Postpartum major depression in remission may represent resolved disease; clinical judgment needed on whether remission codes confirm active diagnosis.

summary: Recall: 78.3% (83 of 106 reference codes retrieved). Precision: 51.2%. F1: 61.9%. 6 reference codes were incorrectly excluded.

scored_codes.term: Major depressive disorder, single episode with atypical features (disorder)

Persistent mood disorders include dysthymia and cyclothymia; dysthymia is a form of primary depression but cyclothymia is not.

scored_codes.term: Recurrent depressive disorder, currently in remission
scored_codes.rationale: Recurrent depressive disorder in remission may represent resolved disease; clinical judgment needed on whether remission codes confirm active diagnosis.
scored_codes.usage_source: NHS Digital HES inpatient FCEs

scored_codes.rationale: Other persistent mood disorders may include dysthymia (primary depression) or other conditions; clinical judgment needed.
scored_codes.usage_source: NHS Digital HES inpatient FCEs

scored_codes.rationale: Postpartum major depression in remission may represent resolved disease; clinical judgment needed on whether remission codes confirm active diagnosis.

scored_codes.rationale: Other mood disorders is a residual category that may include primary depression variants or unrelated conditions.
scored_codes.usage_source: NHS Digital HES inpatient FCEs

scored_codes.rationale: Other single mood disorders is a residual category with unclear clinical scope.
scored_codes.usage_source: NHS Digital HES inpatient FCEs

scored_codes.rationale: Other recurrent mood disorders is a residual category with unclear clinical scope.

scored_codes.term: Excepted from depression quality indicators - informed dissent (finding)
scored_codes.rationale: This is an administrative exception code (informed dissent) that does not confirm the patient has depression; it is a screening/quality indicator exclusion.
scored_codes.usage_source: NHS Digital primary care SNOMED reporting

scored_codes.term: Excepted from depression quality indicators - patient unsuitable (finding)
scored_codes.rationale: This is an administrative exception code (patient unsuitable) that does not confirm the patient has depression; it is a screening/quality indicator exclusion.
scored_codes.usage_source: NHS Digital primary care SNOMED reporting

scored_codes.rationale: Post-schizophrenic depression is a depressive episode secondary to schizophrenia, not primary depression.
Schizoaffective disorder is a distinct primary condition, not primary depression.
scored_codes.usage_source: NHS Digital HES inpatient FCEs

scored_codes.rationale: Bipolar affective disorder is a distinct primary condition, not primary depression.
scored_codes.usage_source: NHS Digital HES inpatient FCEs

scored_codes.term: Bipolar affective disorder, current episode mild or moderate depression
scored_codes.rationale: Bipolar affective disorder with depressive episode is a bipolar condition, not primary depression.
scored_codes.usage_source: NHS Digital HES inpatient FCEs

scored_codes.rationale: Depressive conduct disorder is a childhood behavioral disorder with depression, not primary depression.
scored_codes.usage_source: NHS Digital HES inpatient FCEs

scored_codes.term: Primary degenerative dementia of the Alzheimer type, presenile onset, with depression (disorder)
scored_codes.rationale: Alzheimer dementia with depression is a secondary depressive manifestation of dementia, not primary depression.
scored_codes.usage_source: NHS Digital primary care SNOMED reporting

scored_codes.rationale: Multi-infarct dementia with depression is a secondary depressive manifestation of dementia, not primary depression.
scored_codes.usage_source: NHS Digital primary care SNOMED reporting

scored_codes.rationale: Presenile dementia with depression is a comorbidity pattern where depression is secondary to dementia, not primary depression.
scored_codes.usage_source: NHS Digital primary care SNOMED reporting

scored_codes.rationale: Senile dementia with depression is a comorbidity pattern where depression is secondary to dementia, not primary depression.
scored_codes.usage_source: NHS Digital primary care SNOMED reporting

scored_codes.rationale: Arteriosclerotic dementia with depression is a comorbidity pattern where depression is secondary to dementia, not primary depression.
scored_codes.usage_source: NHS Digital primary care SNOMED reporting

scored_codes.rationale: Depression resolved is a finding indicating the condition is no longer present; resolved conditions should be excluded.
scored_codes.usage_source: NHS Digital primary care SNOMED reporting

scored_codes.term: Primary degenerative dementia of the Alzheimer type, senile onset, with depression (disorder)
scored_codes.rationale: Primary degenerative dementia of the Alzheimer type with depression is a comorbidity pattern where depression is secondary to dementia, not primary depression.
scored_codes.usage_source: NHS Digital primary care SNOMED reporting

scored_codes.rationale: Mixed anxiety and depressive disorder is a distinct primary condition combining anxiety and depression, not primary depression alone.
scored_codes.usage_source: NHS Digital HES inpatient FCEs

scored_codes.term: Bipolar affective disorder, current episode depression (disorder)
scored_codes.rationale: Bipolar affective disorder is a distinct primary psychiatric condition; depressive episodes within bipolar disorder are manifestations of bipolar disorder, not primary unipolar depression.
scored_codes.usage_source: NHS Digital primary care SNOMED reporting

scored_codes.term: Bipolar affective disorder, currently depressed, mild (disorder)
scored_codes.rationale: Bipolar affective disorder, currently depressed is a manifestation of bipolar disorder, not primary unipolar depression.
scored_codes.usage_source: NHS Digital primary care SNOMED reporting

scored_codes.rationale: Unspecified mood disorder is too vague and may include non-depressive conditions.
scored_codes.usage_source: NHS Digital HES inpatient FCEs

scored_codes.term: Single episode of major depression in full remission (disorder)
scored_codes.rationale: Single episode of major depression in full remission describes a resolved state; codes for resolved conditions should typically be excluded.
scored_codes.usage_source: NHS Digital primary care SNOMED reporting

scored_codes.rationale: Major depression in partial remission describes a resolved or resolving state; codes for remission should typically be excluded.
scored_codes.usage_source: NHS Digital primary care SNOMED reporting

scored_codes.rationale: Major depression in remission describes a resolved or resolving state; codes for remission should typically be excluded.
scored_codes.usage_source: NHS Digital primary care SNOMED reporting

scored_codes.rationale: Major depression in full remission describes a resolved state; codes for resolved conditions should typically be excluded.
scored_codes.usage_source: NHS Digital primary care SNOMED reporting
